description: Cover letter hooks by niche — injected as few-shot examples into cover letter generator. 4-5 per niche for variety.

chatbot.label: AI Chatbot / Customer Support
hooks: I noticed you need the bot to handle {detail_from_brief}. I shipped a similar Telegram bot for a news agency — it runs 7 articles/day fully autonomously, zero manual input.
The {feature_from_brief} you described is exactly what my own Telegram bot does in production — 100+ messages/day, context memory, multi-command routing.
A bot that feels natural instead of rigid for {detail_from_brief} — I built exactly this. My production bot handles conversation buffers and remembers context across sessions.
Your team spending {time_wasted} on {manual_task} — I eliminated that exact bottleneck for another client with a conversational AI bot that now runs 24/7 unattended.
Supporting {languages} in a chatbot is something I've already solved — my Telegram bot handles multi-language routing with context management built in.

n8n_automation.label: n8n / Workflow Automation
hooks: Your {process_from_brief} workflow sounds like it needs exactly {technical_detail}. I've built 50+ production n8n workflows including a full lead pipeline that generated 300 qualified leads in 2 weeks.
I've automated exactly this kind of {process_from_brief} before — the last one cut 40 hours/week of manual work down to zero.
The {trigger_detail} to {action_detail} pipeline you described — I run a self-hosted n8n instance with 20+ workflows doing exactly this in production right now.
Turning {manual_process} into a hands-off automation is literally what I do every week. My last n8n build saved a client 15 hours/week and hasn't needed a single manual intervention in 3 months.
I see you need {detail_from_brief} automated end-to-end. I've built this exact pipeline before — n8n + webhooks + error handling, all self-hosted and monitored.

telegram_whatsapp.label: Telegram / WhatsApp Bot
hooks: The {feature_from_brief} you described — appointment reminders, multilingual support — is exactly what I built last month. My own Telegram bot handles 100+ daily messages in production.
I've built a Telegram bot running 24/7 on a dedicated server — {feature_from_brief} is a feature I've already shipped and battle-tested.
A {bot_type} bot with {feature_from_brief} — I run my own in production. It handles conversation context, automated scheduling, and hasn't gone down once in 4 months.
Building bots that feel human is my specialty. My production Telegram bot uses context buffers and multi-command routing — {feature_from_brief} is a natural extension.
I noticed you need {detail_from_brief} on {platform}. I've integrated the Bot API at a deep level — edge cases, rate limits, webhook reliability — all solved in my own production bot.

lead_generation.label: Lead Generation / Scraping
hooks: I see you need {detail_from_brief} — I've built the exact same pipeline for a marketing agency. Delivered 300 qualified leads in 2 weeks, fully automated with n8n + enrichment.
Getting {detail_from_brief} is one of my most common builds — last one ran for 3 months straight without a single manual intervention.
Scraping {source} and enriching with {enrichment_type} — I built a Chrome extension + pipeline that does exactly this. Currently scoring and qualifying leads automatically for my own CRM.
Your team doing {manual_task} manually — I automated that exact workflow for another client. Pipeline runs daily: scrape, clean, enrich, push to CRM. Zero human touch.
I noticed you need {volume} leads from {source}. I've built pipelines that deliver this volume weekly — automated extraction, dedup, enrichment, and direct CRM injection.

api_integration.label: API Integration / Make.com / Webhooks
hooks: Connecting {api_a} to {api_b} with {condition_from_brief} is one of my most common builds. I've integrated 12+ APIs in production — Claude, Gmail, Telegram, Supabase, Cloudflare, government APIs.
I've built this exact kind of {integration_type} integration — clean architecture, error handling, retry logic, and monitoring included.
Syncing {system_a} with {system_b} in real-time — I built a similar webhook pipeline that processes thousands of events daily with zero data loss.
The {api_detail} integration you described — I recently shipped a similar API connector for a production system. Error handling, rate limiting, and logging all built in from day one.

I noticed you need {detail_from_brief}. I've connected APIs that are much harder to work with — government cadastre, legal document generation, real-time financial data. Your integration is well within scope.

rag_knowledge.label: RAG / Knowledge Base / AI Search
hooks: I run a production RAG system with hybrid search — ONNX embeddings + BM25 + knowledge graph with temporal decay. Your {use_case} is a direct fit for this architecture.
Building {use_case} with RAG is exactly what I do daily — my own system processes thousands of documents with hybrid search and PageRank scoring.
The {document_type} retrieval system you need — I built one for legal documents that generates answers in <30 seconds with 85-92% accuracy. Same architecture applies here.
I noticed you need AI that actually understands your {document_type}. I've built production RAG with ONNX embeddings, BM25 fallback, and knowledge graphs — not just a basic vector store.
Your {use_case} needs more than simple embedding search. I built a hybrid retrieval system with temporal decay scoring that prioritizes recent and relevant content — exactly what you're describing.

generic.label: Generic (fallback)
hooks: I noticed {detail_from_brief} — I've built something very similar for {client_type}. Shipped in {timeframe}, still running in production today.
The {detail_from_brief} you described is a problem I've solved before — {result_achieved} for a similar client.
I see you need {detail_from_brief}. I shipped a comparable system that's been running in production for months — same stack, same challenges, proven solution.
Your {pain_point} — I eliminated that exact problem for another client. The solution has been running hands-off for {timeframe}.
I noticed {detail_from_brief}. This is my daily work — I run 38 AI agents in production and have shipped 50+ similar automations.

injection_rules.instruction: Use these hooks as INSPIRATION — adapt the structure and tone but fill in with REAL details from the job brief. NEVER copy a hook word-for-word.
